questions.prompt: A merchant traveling from Cairo to Beijing around 1300 would most likely have encountered which political entity controlling much of the overland route between Central Asia and northern China?
choices: The Yuan Dynasty established by Kublai Khan
The Tang Dynasty under Emperor Taizong
The Han Dynasty bureaucratic state
The Sui Dynasty canal-building regime
questions.correctText: The Yuan Dynasty established by Kublai Khan
questions.explanation: Kublai Khan founded the Yuan Dynasty in 1271 after Mongol conquest of Song China, governing all of China until 1368. The Mongol Pax Mongolica made the route across Central Asia comparatively safe for traders like the contemporary Marco Polo.

questions.prompt: Ibn Battuta's account of his visit to the court of Mansa Sulayman in Mali in the 1350s emphasized the strict justice, prosperity, and Muslim piety he observed. This passage best illustrates which broader development in this period?
choices: The decline of trans-Saharan trade following the Black Death
European colonial penetration of the West African interior
The spread of Islam along Afro-Eurasian trade networks into Sub-Saharan Africa
The replacement of Arabic with Mande as the language of West African scholarship
questions.correctText: The spread of Islam along Afro-Eurasian trade networks into Sub-Saharan Africa
questions.explanation: By the 14th century, Mali's ruling elite had adopted Islam through trans-Saharan trade connections, and Mansa Musa's earlier 1324 hajj had made the empire famous across the Islamic world. Sub-Saharan kingdoms commonly blended Islamic practice with local traditions.

questions.prompt: Which feature most distinguished the political organization of the Aztec Empire under Moctezuma II from that of contemporary European feudal kingdoms around 1500?
choices: Reliance on a tribute system from subordinate city-states rather than land-based vassalage
Use of written law codes derived from Roman precedents
Hereditary monarchy passed from father to eldest son
Maintenance of large permanent professional standing armies
questions.correctText: Reliance on a tribute system from subordinate city-states rather than land-based vassalage
questions.explanation: The Aztec Triple Alliance extracted tribute (food, textiles, sacrificial victims) from conquered altepetl rather than imposing direct administration, contrasting with European fief-and-vassal arrangements tied to landholding.

questions.prompt: The construction of grand stone enclosures at Great Zimbabwe between approximately 1250 and 1450 provides evidence of which broader pattern in Southeast Africa during this period?
choices: Christian missionary architecture spreading from Ethiopia
Wealth accumulation from controlling gold and ivory trade to Indian Ocean ports
Bantu migrations finally reaching the Cape of Good Hope
Portuguese feitoria construction along the Swahili coast
questions.correctText: Wealth accumulation from controlling gold and ivory trade to Indian Ocean ports
questions.explanation: Great Zimbabwe's elites grew wealthy by funneling interior gold and ivory toward coastal Swahili city-states like Kilwa and Sofala, which connected to the wider Indian Ocean trading world. The Portuguese arrived only after the site's decline.

questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the relationship between Song Dynasty China (960-1279) and its surrounding tributary states such as Korea (Goryeo) and Vietnam (Dai Viet)?
choices: These states formally rejected Chinese culture and writing systems
They participated in a ritual hierarchy in which they sent tribute and adopted Confucian governance models while retaining political autonomy
They were directly governed by Chinese-appointed magistrates
They formed military alliances against the Song that succeeded in conquering Kaifeng
questions.correctText: They participated in a ritual hierarchy in which they sent tribute and adopted Confucian governance models while retaining political autonomy
questions.explanation: The Chinese tributary system allowed neighboring states to acknowledge Chinese cultural and ritual primacy by sending periodic embassies and gifts while keeping their own dynasties and laws. Korea, Vietnam, and Japan all adopted Confucian examinations and Chinese script.

questions.prompt: The travel narratives of Marco Polo (returning to Venice in 1295) and Ibn Battuta (writing in the 1350s) both reflect which key feature of Afro-Eurasian connections in this period?
choices: The deliberate isolation of major civilizations from one another
The dominance of European technological superiority over Asian societies
Long-distance travel made possible by integrated land and sea networks linking diverse cultures
The complete disappearance of overland routes after the rise of maritime trade
questions.correctText: Long-distance travel made possible by integrated land and sea networks linking diverse cultures
questions.explanation: Both travelers crossed vast multilingual networks — Polo over the Silk Roads under Mongol protection, Ibn Battuta across Dar al-Islam by camel caravan and dhow. Their accounts document a deeply interconnected pre-Columbian world.

questions.prompt: The catastrophic spread of bubonic plague across Eurasia and North Africa from approximately 1346 to 1353 was most directly enabled by which development?
choices: European voyages of exploration to the Americas
The opening of the Suez Canal to maritime traffic
The Columbian Exchange between Old and New Worlds
Intensified caravan and maritime trade along the Mongol-secured Silk Roads and the Mediterranean
questions.correctText: Intensified caravan and maritime trade along the Mongol-secured Silk Roads and the Mediterranean
questions.explanation: Yersinia pestis spread westward from Central Asia along trade routes integrated by the Mongol Pax Mongolica, reaching Crimea in 1346 and Italy by 1347 via Genoese ships. The plague killed roughly one-third of Europe's population.

questions.prompt: The development of larger ships such as the Chinese junk and Arab dhow, as well as advances in navigational tools like the magnetic compass and astrolabe, had which collective effect during the 1200-1450 period?
choices: Expansion of Indian Ocean trade volume and the rise of commercial cities like Calicut, Malacca, and Kilwa
The decline of long-distance trade in favor of localized economies
European naval dominance throughout the Indian Ocean basin
The complete abandonment of overland trade routes
questions.correctText: Expansion of Indian Ocean trade volume and the rise of commercial cities like Calicut, Malacca, and Kilwa
questions.explanation: Improved shipbuilding and navigation allowed merchants to ride seasonal monsoon winds across the Indian Ocean basin, enriching port cities from the Swahili coast to Southeast Asia long before Portuguese arrival in 1498.

questions.prompt: Zheng He's seven voyages between 1405 and 1433, undertaken on behalf of the Ming Yongle Emperor, are best understood as serving which primary purpose?
choices: Establishing permanent Chinese colonies in East Africa
Replacing Arab and Indian merchants in the spice trade
Asserting Ming prestige and incorporating distant kingdoms into China's tributary system
Discovering a sea route to the Americas
questions.correctText: Asserting Ming prestige and incorporating distant kingdoms into China's tributary system
questions.explanation: Zheng He's massive treasure fleets visited Southeast Asia, India, Arabia, and the Swahili coast to display Ming power and bring foreign envoys back to pay tribute. The voyages ended after 1433 when the Confucian bureaucracy redirected resources to land defense.

questions.prompt: Which development best illustrates the diffusion of technology along Afro-Eurasian trade networks between 1200 and 1450?
choices: The transfer of Chinese gunpowder, paper, and printing technologies westward to the Islamic world and Europe
The introduction of the alphabet from Mesoamerica into Asia
The replacement of Roman concrete with bamboo-based construction in Europe
The diffusion of New World maize into the Mediterranean by 1300
questions.correctText: The transfer of Chinese gunpowder, paper, and printing technologies westward to the Islamic world and Europe
questions.explanation: Gunpowder, papermaking, the magnetic compass, and movable type all spread from Song-era China westward through Islamic intermediaries during the 12th-14th centuries. These transfers reshaped warfare, learning, and commerce in the receiving regions.

questions.prompt: The Ottoman, Safavid, and Mughal empires are often grouped together as the 'Gunpowder Empires' because they shared which common feature?
choices: Conversion of their entire populations to Sunni Islam
Adoption of parliamentary government modeled on the English Parliament
Use of artillery and firearms by centralized armies to conquer and consolidate vast multiethnic territories
Establishment of overseas plantation colonies in the Americas
questions.correctText: Use of artillery and firearms by centralized armies to conquer and consolidate vast multiethnic territories
questions.explanation: Ottoman cannons breached Constantinople's walls in 1453, Safavid muskets defeated Uzbeks, and Mughal artillery enabled Babur's 1526 victory at Panipat. Centralized control of expensive gunpowder weapons strengthened imperial monarchs against regional nobles.

questions.prompt: Sultan Suleiman I (r. 1520-1566) earned the epithet 'the Lawgiver' in the Ottoman tradition primarily because of which achievement?
choices: He abolished the Janissary corps in favor of a citizen militia
He codified secular kanun laws to operate alongside Islamic sharia across the empire
He converted the Ottoman state to Twelver Shi'ism
He drafted a written constitution limiting the powers of the sultan
questions.correctText: He codified secular kanun laws to operate alongside Islamic sharia across the empire
questions.explanation: Suleiman issued comprehensive kanun decrees covering taxation, criminal procedure, and land tenure, harmonizing them with religious law. Europeans called him 'the Magnificent' for his military conquests; Ottomans honored him as Kanuni, 'the Lawgiver.'

questions.prompt: Akbar the Great of the Mughal Empire (r. 1556-1605) pursued which policy toward his religiously diverse subjects?
choices: Forced conversion of all Hindus to Islam
Expulsion of all non-Muslims from Mughal territory
Religious toleration, abolition of the jizya tax on non-Muslims, and inclusion of Hindu Rajputs in the imperial administration
Establishment of Christianity as the state religion
questions.correctText: Religious toleration, abolition of the jizya tax on non-Muslims, and inclusion of Hindu Rajputs in the imperial administration
questions.explanation: Akbar married Rajput princesses, eliminated the jizya in 1564, sponsored interfaith debates at his court at Fatehpur Sikri, and even founded a syncretic 'Din-i-Ilahi' religion. Later emperor Aurangzeb reversed these policies, helping fuel Mughal decline.

questions.prompt: Which factor most strongly explains the Manchu Qing Dynasty's ability to govern Han Chinese populations after the conquest of 1644?
choices: Complete dismantling of the Confucian civil service examination system
Mass deportation of the Han majority to Manchuria
Adoption of Han Chinese bureaucratic institutions while preserving Manchu cultural and military distinctiveness
Conversion of the Han Chinese population to Tibetan Buddhism
questions.correctText: Adoption of Han Chinese bureaucratic institutions while preserving Manchu cultural and military distinctiveness
questions.explanation: The Qing kept the Confucian examination system, employed Han officials in a dual structure with Manchus, and patronized Confucian scholarship, while requiring the queue hairstyle and reserving the Eight Banners military system for Manchus. This dual administration sustained the dynasty until 1912.

questions.prompt: Russian Tsar Peter I (r. 1682-1725) launched programs of 'Westernization' including the construction of St. Petersburg, the introduction of European-style military training, and the requirement that nobles shave their beards. What was Peter's primary motivation?
choices: To establish Russia as a major European power capable of competing militarily and economically with states like Sweden and France
To convert Russia from Orthodox Christianity to Roman Catholicism
To dissolve serfdom and create a free peasantry
To withdraw Russia from involvement with Western European affairs
questions.correctText: To establish Russia as a major European power capable of competing militarily and economically with states like Sweden and France
questions.explanation: Peter's 1697-98 'Grand Embassy' to Western Europe convinced him that Russia needed modern shipyards, a navy, and a reformed army to compete. His victory over Sweden in the Great Northern War (1721) established Russia's Baltic empire and 'European' standing.

questions.prompt: The Portuguese voyages of exploration in the 15th century, including Bartolomeu Dias's 1488 rounding of the Cape of Good Hope and Vasco da Gama's 1498 arrival at Calicut, were most directly motivated by which combination of factors?
choices: A desire to reach the Pacific Ocean and find a route to North America
Plans to establish coffee and sugar plantations in tropical Africa
A desire to bypass Italian and Muslim intermediaries in the spice trade and to spread Christianity
The need to relieve population pressure caused by industrialization
questions.correctText: A desire to bypass Italian and Muslim intermediaries in the spice trade and to spread Christianity
questions.explanation: Henry the Navigator's school at Sagres pursued an alternate route to Asian spices, while papal bulls authorized Portuguese conquest of 'heathen' lands. Da Gama announced his goal in Calicut as 'Christians and spices.'

questions.prompt: The Columbian Exchange transformed both the Americas and Afro-Eurasia after 1492. Which pairing represents two of its most demographically significant consequences?
choices: European adoption of American maize and potatoes alongside catastrophic disease deaths among Indigenous populations
American adoption of the wheel and Eurasian adoption of pyramidal stone construction
Disappearance of horses from the Americas and introduction of bison to Europe
Decline of European populations and population growth among Indigenous Americans
questions.correctText: European adoption of American maize and potatoes alongside catastrophic disease deaths among Indigenous populations
questions.explanation: Old World pathogens like smallpox killed an estimated 75-90% of the Indigenous population over the 16th century, while New World crops fed surging Old World populations. By 1750 potatoes had become a staple from Ireland to Russia.

questions.prompt: The Spanish encomienda system in the early colonial Americas operated in which way?
choices: It granted self-government to Indigenous communities free from Spanish oversight
It established public schools for Indigenous children in Castilian Spanish
It awarded Spanish colonists rights to extract labor and tribute from designated Indigenous communities in exchange for nominal 'protection' and Christian instruction
It compensated Indigenous workers with wages equal to those of Spanish laborers
questions.correctText: It awarded Spanish colonists rights to extract labor and tribute from designated Indigenous communities in exchange for nominal 'protection' and Christian instruction
questions.explanation: The encomienda functioned as coerced labor extraction by Spanish encomenderos. The 1542 New Laws sought to curb its abuses after critiques by Bartolomé de las Casas; the system gradually gave way to repartimiento and the mita.

questions.prompt: The transatlantic slave trade, which forcibly transported an estimated 12.5 million Africans to the Americas between roughly 1500 and 1867, expanded most dramatically in connection with which development?
choices: Industrial textile manufacturing in 18th-century Manchester
The shift from indentured servitude to large-scale enslaved African labor on Caribbean and Brazilian sugar plantations during the 17th century
The construction of canals in central Africa
Roman Catholic missionary expeditions into Central Asia
questions.correctText: The shift from indentured servitude to large-scale enslaved African labor on Caribbean and Brazilian sugar plantations during the 17th century
questions.explanation: As sugar plantations expanded in Barbados, Saint-Domingue, and Brazil, demand for coerced labor surged. African captives — purchased from coastal kingdoms like Dahomey and Asante — replaced indentured Europeans and Indigenous laborers.

questions.prompt: The 'Manila galleon' trade between Acapulco and the Philippines, operating from 1565 to 1815, is most significant for illustrating which global pattern?
choices: The first regular maritime link between the Americas and Asia, exchanging American silver for Chinese silks and porcelain
Spanish naval dominance over the Atlantic slave trade
Portuguese monopoly over the Indian Ocean spice trade
British East India Company control of Chinese tea exports
questions.correctText: The first regular maritime link between the Americas and Asia, exchanging American silver for Chinese silks and porcelain
questions.explanation: Galleons carried Potosí silver west to Manila, where Chinese merchants exchanged it for luxury goods that returned to Mexico and Spain. This trade integrated Ming China into a truly global silver economy.

questions.prompt: The Haitian Revolution (1791-1804), led at various points by Toussaint Louverture and Jean-Jacques Dessalines, is historically significant for which reason?
choices: It restored French colonial rule over the island and reinstated slavery permanently
It produced the only successful large-scale revolt of enslaved people and the second independent republic in the Western Hemisphere
It established the first absolute monarchy in the Caribbean
It united Saint-Domingue with the Spanish Empire under a Bourbon king
questions.correctText: It produced the only successful large-scale revolt of enslaved people and the second independent republic in the Western Hemisphere
questions.explanation: Enslaved Africans and free people of color defeated Napoleon's expeditionary force and declared Haitian independence on January 1, 1804. The revolution terrified slaveholders elsewhere and prompted Napoleon's sale of Louisiana to the United States.

questions.prompt: Mary Wollstonecraft's 1792 work 'A Vindication of the Rights of Woman' applied Enlightenment ideas in which way?
choices: It argued that women should be excluded from formal education to preserve domestic virtue
It defended slavery as compatible with natural rights philosophy
It argued that natural rights and the capacity for reason applied equally to women, who therefore deserved education and civic participation
It called for the restoration of absolute monarchy across Europe
questions.correctText: It argued that natural rights and the capacity for reason applied equally to women, who therefore deserved education and civic participation
questions.explanation: Wollstonecraft responded to the French Revolution's expansive rhetoric about citizenship by demanding the same rights for women. Her work became foundational for later feminist movements, including the 1848 Seneca Falls Convention.

questions.prompt: Simón Bolívar, who led independence campaigns across northern South America between 1810 and 1825, drew most directly on which intellectual tradition to justify revolution against Spanish rule?
choices: Confucian philosophy emphasizing filial obedience
Medieval scholasticism rejecting natural law
Enlightenment political theory, especially ideas of popular sovereignty drawn from thinkers like Montesquieu and Rousseau
Divine right of kings as articulated by Bishop Bossuet
questions.correctText: Enlightenment political theory, especially ideas of popular sovereignty drawn from thinkers like Montesquieu and Rousseau
questions.explanation: Bolívar's 1815 Jamaica Letter and his Angostura Address invoked republicanism and separation of powers. Creole elites across Spanish America cited the American and French Revolutions, and Napoleon's 1808 seizure of Spain provided the political opening.

questions.prompt: The unifications of Italy (completed in 1871) and Germany (completed in 1871) shared which essential characteristic?
choices: Both produced absolute monarchies with no representative institutions
Both abolished private property and established communist states
Both replaced patchworks of small states with centralized nation-states forged largely through diplomacy and war led by figures like Cavour and Bismarck
Both resulted from peaceful federation without military conflict
questions.correctText: Both replaced patchworks of small states with centralized nation-states forged largely through diplomacy and war led by figures like Cavour and Bismarck
questions.explanation: Cavour orchestrated Piedmont's leadership in Italian unification through wars with Austria; Bismarck used three wars (1864, 1866, 1870-71) to forge a German Empire under Prussian leadership. Both exemplify the rise of European nationalism.

questions.prompt: The Taiping Rebellion in China (1850-1864), led by Hong Xiuquan, who claimed to be the younger brother of Jesus Christ, is best understood as which of the following?
choices: A peaceful Confucian reform movement supported by the Qing court
A massive internal upheaval combining religious millenarianism, peasant grievances, and ethnic resentment against Manchu rule, killing an estimated 20-30 million people
A foreign invasion launched by European missionaries
A successful revolution that ended the Qing Dynasty and established a republic
questions.correctText: A massive internal upheaval combining religious millenarianism, peasant grievances, and ethnic resentment against Manchu rule, killing an estimated 20-30 million people
questions.explanation: Hong's syncretic Christian-inspired Heavenly Kingdom captured Nanjing in 1853 and was suppressed only after years of fighting led by provincial Qing armies under figures like Zeng Guofan. The rebellion gravely weakened the dynasty even though it survived.

questions.prompt: Which combination of factors best explains why industrialization began in Britain rather than elsewhere in the late 18th century?
choices: Abundant coal and iron deposits, navigable rivers, Atlantic trade profits, stable government, and an enclosure-driven agricultural surplus that freed labor for manufacturing
A large population of nomadic herders and the absence of private property
Strict government prohibitions on mechanical invention
The discovery of petroleum in Yorkshire in 1750
questions.correctText: Abundant coal and iron deposits, navigable rivers, Atlantic trade profits, stable government, and an enclosure-driven agricultural surplus that freed labor for manufacturing
questions.explanation: Historians like Kenneth Pomeranz and Joel Mokyr point to this convergence: cheap fossil energy near the surface, Atlantic-economy capital, a constitutional state, and surplus labor created by enclosure made Britain the first industrial society.

questions.prompt: Friedrich Engels's 1845 work 'The Condition of the Working Class in England' described overcrowded slums, child labor, and dangerous factory conditions in Manchester. This source most directly supports which broader argument about industrial capitalism?
choices: That industrialization produced rapid economic growth but also severe inequality and harsh urban living conditions for laborers
That early industrial workers consistently received generous wages and benefits
That Britain rejected industrialization in favor of agriculture
That industrialization had no measurable effect on European cities
questions.correctText: That industrialization produced rapid economic growth but also severe inequality and harsh urban living conditions for laborers
questions.explanation: Engels documented squalid conditions to support his and Marx's critique of industrial capitalism. Their 1848 Communist Manifesto called for proletarian revolution; reformist responses produced factory acts, unions, and eventually welfare states.

questions.prompt: The Berlin Conference of 1884-1885 is significant in the history of European imperialism for which reason?
choices: It granted independence to all African colonies
It established rules among European powers for claiming African territory, including the principle of 'effective occupation,' and accelerated the Scramble for Africa
It abolished the transatlantic slave trade
It formed a permanent African union of independent states
questions.correctText: It established rules among European powers for claiming African territory, including the principle of 'effective occupation,' and accelerated the Scramble for Africa
questions.explanation: Convened by Bismarck, the conference produced the General Act dividing Africa among European powers without African representation. By 1914, only Ethiopia and Liberia remained free of colonial rule.

questions.prompt: The Meiji Restoration in Japan (beginning 1868) responded to the threat of Western imperialism by pursuing which strategy?
choices: Rejecting all foreign technology and isolating Japan more strictly than under the Tokugawa
Adopting Western industrial technology, military organization, and constitutional government while preserving the imperial throne and selected Japanese traditions
Inviting European powers to colonize Japanese territory
Establishing communist economic planning under the emperor
questions.correctText: Adopting Western industrial technology, military organization, and constitutional government while preserving the imperial throne and selected Japanese traditions
questions.explanation: The Iwakura Mission of 1871-73 surveyed Western institutions; subsequent reforms built railroads, conscription armies, and a Prussian-style 1889 constitution. Japan defeated China in 1895 and Russia in 1905, becoming the first non-Western great power.

questions.prompt: The 1857 Indian Rebellion (the 'Sepoy Mutiny') against the British East India Company is best understood as resulting from which combination of grievances?
choices: Indian dissatisfaction with British religious tolerance and economic generosity
Sepoys' objection to greased cartridges, broader Indian resentment over the doctrine of lapse, missionary activity, and economic disruption
Demands by Indian princes for Britain to expand the East India Company's authority
A peasant movement to restore Portuguese colonial rule
questions.correctText: Sepoys' objection to greased cartridges, broader Indian resentment over the doctrine of lapse, missionary activity, and economic disruption
questions.explanation: Hindu and Muslim sepoys believed cartridges were greased with cow and pig fat, but underlying causes ran deeper. After suppressing the revolt, the British Crown abolished the Company in 1858 and ruled India directly as the Raj.

questions.prompt: The system of pre-1914 European alliances — the Triple Entente (France, Russia, Britain) and the Triple Alliance (Germany, Austria-Hungary, Italy) — contributed to the outbreak of World War I in which way?
choices: By guaranteeing that any localized conflict would remain limited to two combatants
By turning a regional Balkan crisis after Archduke Franz Ferdinand's assassination into a continent-wide war
By eliminating arms races among the great powers
By transferring control of European foreign policy to the United States
questions.correctText: By turning a regional Balkan crisis after Archduke Franz Ferdinand's assassination into a continent-wide war
questions.explanation: When Austria-Hungary declared war on Serbia after the June 28, 1914 assassination in Sarajevo, the alliance system pulled Russia, Germany, France, and Britain into war within weeks. Pre-war planning like the Schlieffen Plan made mobilization especially rigid.

questions.prompt: The Treaty of Versailles of 1919 contained which key provisions regarding Germany?
choices: Restoration of all German colonies and full rearmament rights
The 'war guilt' Article 231, heavy reparations, loss of overseas colonies, and strict limits on the German military
Permanent German occupation of Belgium and northern France
Recognition of Wilhelm II as emperor of a Greater German Empire
questions.correctText: The 'war guilt' Article 231, heavy reparations, loss of overseas colonies, and strict limits on the German military
questions.explanation: The treaty's harsh terms fueled widespread German resentment that Nazi propaganda later exploited. Economist John Maynard Keynes immediately warned in 'The Economic Consequences of the Peace' that the settlement was destabilizing.

questions.prompt: Joseph Stalin's Five-Year Plans, beginning in 1928, aimed primarily at which goal?
choices: Rapidly industrializing the Soviet economy through state-owned heavy industry and forced collectivization of agriculture
Restoring private property and a free-market economy
Encouraging Soviet citizens to emigrate to Western Europe
Eliminating all Soviet trade with foreign countries
questions.correctText: Rapidly industrializing the Soviet economy through state-owned heavy industry and forced collectivization of agriculture
questions.explanation: Stalin's plans built steel, coal, and machinery industries while forcibly creating collective farms. Collectivization caused mass famine, especially in Ukraine (the Holodomor) where millions died between 1932 and 1933.

questions.prompt: The Indian National Congress, under leaders like Mohandas Gandhi and Jawaharlal Nehru, pursued independence from Britain through which primary strategy in the 1920s and 1930s?
choices: Mass nonviolent civil disobedience, including boycotts of British goods and acts like the 1930 Salt March
A guerrilla insurgency modeled on the French Resistance
Annexation of British India into the Ottoman Caliphate
Petitions exclusively addressed to the U.S. Congress
questions.correctText: Mass nonviolent civil disobedience, including boycotts of British goods and acts like the 1930 Salt March
questions.explanation: Gandhi's strategy of satyagraha mobilized millions in mass protests. The 240-mile Salt March of March-April 1930 deliberately broke the British salt monopoly and became a global symbol of anticolonial resistance.

questions.prompt: The Nazi policy known as the Holocaust resulted in the murder of approximately how many people, and primarily targeted which group?
choices: Approximately 6 million European Jews, plus millions of Roma, disabled people, Slavs, and political prisoners
Approximately 6,000 American soldiers in a single battle
All non-German civilians in occupied France
Approximately 60 million people, almost all of them Japanese civilians
questions.correctText: Approximately 6 million European Jews, plus millions of Roma, disabled people, Slavs, and political prisoners
questions.explanation: From the 1935 Nuremberg Laws through industrialized killing at camps like Auschwitz-Birkenau, the Nazi regime systematically murdered Jews and other groups deemed 'undesirable.' The 1945 liberation of camps revealed the full scale to a horrified world.

questions.prompt: The Truman Doctrine, announced by President Harry Truman in March 1947, committed the United States to which course of action?
choices: Returning to isolationism and withdrawing from European affairs
Containing communist expansion by supporting nations resisting communist takeover, beginning with aid to Greece and Turkey
Forming a permanent military alliance with the Soviet Union
Dissolving NATO before it could be founded
questions.correctText: Containing communist expansion by supporting nations resisting communist takeover, beginning with aid to Greece and Turkey
questions.explanation: Drawing on George Kennan's 'Long Telegram,' Truman pledged support to 'free peoples who are resisting attempted subjugation.' This containment policy guided U.S. Cold War strategy through the Marshall Plan, NATO, and conflicts in Korea and Vietnam.

questions.prompt: The Cuban Missile Crisis of October 1962 is widely considered the closest the Cold War came to direct nuclear conflict. The crisis was resolved when:
choices: The United States invaded Cuba and overthrew Fidel Castro
Khrushchev agreed to remove Soviet missiles from Cuba in exchange for a U.S. pledge not to invade and the eventual removal of U.S. missiles from Turkey
Soviet missiles were launched at Florida and intercepted by U.S. forces
China brokered a permanent military alliance between Cuba and the United States
questions.correctText: Khrushchev agreed to remove Soviet missiles from Cuba in exchange for a U.S. pledge not to invade and the eventual removal of U.S. missiles from Turkey
questions.explanation: After a 13-day standoff involving a U.S. naval quarantine, the Kennedy-Khrushchev deal defused the crisis. The episode prompted the 1963 Limited Test Ban Treaty and the Moscow-Washington hotline.

questions.prompt: Mao Zedong's Great Leap Forward (1958-1962) attempted to industrialize China rapidly through which approach?
choices: Forming joint ventures with U.S. and British corporations
Mass mobilization of peasants into people's communes and backyard steel furnaces, with disastrous results including famine that killed tens of millions
Privatization of state-owned enterprises
Importing all heavy industrial equipment from the United States
questions.correctText: Mass mobilization of peasants into people's communes and backyard steel furnaces, with disastrous results including famine that killed tens of millions
questions.explanation: Mao's campaign collectivized peasants and demanded unrealistic grain quotas. Yields collapsed, but local officials reported phantom successes, leading to mass starvation between 1959 and 1961 — among the deadliest famines in history.

questions.prompt: The 1947 partition of British India created which two new states, and what was its immediate human cost?
choices: India and Pakistan, with roughly 14 million people displaced and as many as one to two million killed in communal violence
India and Bangladesh, created peacefully and without significant population movement
Pakistan and Sri Lanka, with no resulting displacement
India and Nepal, with full unification of all Hindus and Muslims in a single state
questions.correctText: India and Pakistan, with roughly 14 million people displaced and as many as one to two million killed in communal violence
questions.explanation: The Radcliffe Line, drawn hastily in summer 1947, separated Hindu-majority India from Muslim-majority Pakistan. The partition produced one of the largest forced migrations in human history; Bangladesh would later split from Pakistan in 1971.

questions.prompt: Nelson Mandela became South Africa's first Black president in 1994 after which decades-long political process?
choices: A swift military coup against the apartheid government

Decades of struggle against apartheid by the African National Congress, including Mandela's 27 years of imprisonment, followed by negotiations under F. W. de Klerk and South Africa's first multiracial elections
A unilateral declaration of independence from the United Kingdom in 1965
A successful conquest by neighboring African states
questions.correctText: Decades of struggle against apartheid by the African National Congress, including Mandela's 27 years of imprisonment, followed by negotiations under F. W. de Klerk and South Africa's first multiracial elections
questions.explanation: Mandela was released from Robben Island in 1990. The 1994 elections ended legal apartheid; Mandela and de Klerk shared the 1993 Nobel Peace Prize, and the Truth and Reconciliation Commission afterward addressed apartheid-era abuses.

questions.prompt: The collapse of the Soviet Union in December 1991 was preceded by which set of reforms launched by Mikhail Gorbachev?
choices: Stalin-style purges and renewed state control of agriculture
Perestroika (economic restructuring) and glasnost (political openness), which inadvertently accelerated reform demands within Soviet republics
A full restoration of Tsarist autocracy
Complete military reoccupation of Eastern European satellite states
questions.correctText: Perestroika (economic restructuring) and glasnost (political openness), which inadvertently accelerated reform demands within Soviet republics
questions.explanation: Gorbachev's reforms after 1985 sought to revitalize a stagnant Soviet economy but unleashed nationalism in Baltic and Caucasian republics. The Berlin Wall fell in 1989, and 15 successor states declared independence by the end of 1991.

questions.prompt: The 1994 Rwandan genocide, in which an estimated 800,000 people were killed in approximately 100 days, was carried out primarily by which group and against which group?
choices: Belgian colonists against Indigenous Africans
Hutu extremists, organized by the Rwandan government and the Interahamwe militia, against Tutsi civilians and moderate Hutus
United Nations peacekeepers against Hutu civilians
Tutsi commanders against Belgian missionaries
questions.correctText: Hutu extremists, organized by the Rwandan government and the Interahamwe militia, against Tutsi civilians and moderate Hutus
questions.explanation: Colonial-era ethnic categories sharpened by Belgian rule were weaponized after the April 6, 1994 assassination of President Habyarimana. The international community's failure to intervene became a touchstone in debates over the responsibility to protect.

questions.prompt: The Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), formed by the United Nations in 1988, has reached which scientific consensus regarding contemporary climate change?
choices: That recent warming is entirely the result of natural solar cycles
That human activity, especially the burning of fossil fuels and resulting greenhouse-gas emissions, is the dominant cause of observed global warming since the mid-20th century
That global temperatures have fallen since 1900
That climate change is occurring only in the Southern Hemisphere
questions.correctText: That human activity, especially the burning of fossil fuels and resulting greenhouse-gas emissions, is the dominant cause of observed global warming since the mid-20th century
questions.explanation: Successive IPCC Assessment Reports (most recently AR6) document this consensus. The 1997 Kyoto Protocol and 2015 Paris Agreement attempted to coordinate international responses, though enforcement and emission reductions remain politically contentious.

questions.prompt: The 1995 founding of the World Trade Organization (WTO), the 1992 Maastricht Treaty creating the European Union, and the 1994 North American Free Trade Agreement (NAFTA) collectively reflect which broader development?
choices: A turn toward economic autarky and the dismantling of trade agreements
The deepening of post-Cold War economic globalization through trade liberalization and regional integration
The end of all national currencies
A return to mercantilist policies as the dominant global economic framework
questions.correctText: The deepening of post-Cold War economic globalization through trade liberalization and regional integration
questions.explanation: These agreements lowered tariffs, harmonized regulations, and integrated regional markets. While globalization spurred growth, it also generated backlash visible in protests at the 1999 WTO Seattle meeting and later populist movements.

questions.prompt: Which technological development has most reshaped global communication, commerce, and political mobilization since approximately 1990?
choices: The invention of the printing press
The completion of the transcontinental railroad
The rapid spread of the personal computer, the internet, and mobile devices, enabling near-instant global information exchange
The development of the steam engine
questions.correctText: The rapid spread of the personal computer, the internet, and mobile devices, enabling near-instant global information exchange
questions.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee's 1989 invention of the World Wide Web and the subsequent proliferation of smartphones since the 2007 iPhone launch have transformed everything from supply chains to political organizing, exemplified by movements like the 2010-11 Arab Spring.
